codeReview.summary: The commit adds new modules and substantial tests, but key required behaviors are not fully implemented: fee collection is not integrated into live trade settlement, delegated subaccounts are not wired into order placement/cancellation entry paths, and the get_user_balances view API does not match the feature contract signature.

issues.description: Trades settle by transferring full quote amount directly between buyer/seller (lines 96-106) with no fee calculation or vault collection calls. This misses the required behavior that non-zero maker/taker fees are collected on trades.

Order entry functions are signer-only and operate on signer::address_of(user), with no delegation/owner resolution via subaccounts::assert_authorized_trader. Required behavior says delegated addresses can place orders on behalf of owner.
cancel_order authorizes strictly by order owner == signer::address_of(user). There is no delegated-caller path, so delegated addresses cannot cancel on behalf of owner as required.

get_user_balances signature requires (user_addr, base_asset_addr, quote_asset_addr). The feature contract specifies get_user_balances(user_addr) returning available/locked per asset, so the public interface does not match the required API.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The move-worker procedure appears to lack an explicit integration checkpoint for cross-module features (e.g., ensuring new authorization/fee modules are actually invoked from existing entry/settlement flows), which allowed implementation to stop at module creation plus unit tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: In commit 114f195, subaccounts defines assert_authorized_trader (contracts/sources/subaccounts.move:140) but there are no subaccounts:: call sites in contract entry modules; settlement has no fees:: usage while feature required fee collection on trades.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported recurring friend-declaration friction while integrating new modules; documenting this explicitly in move-worker could reduce avoidable compile-debug cycles.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges in /Users/maxmohammadi/.factory/missions/79476079-9b4c-40cb-b69e-9e85801c6056/handoffs/2026-03-30T23-39-09-136Z__contract-views-fees-edge-cases__f682084a-2090-4ec6-96b1-f5fc26b7b45b.json requests adding guidance on friend declarations.

summary: Reviewed feature contract-views-fees-edge-cases using features.json, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 114f195 diff. Despite extensive new code/tests, the implementation does not satisfy critical expected behaviors for trade-fee integration and delegated trading/cancellation, and the balances view API shape diverges from the specified contract, so the review result is fail.
